summary: Loads image or video files (PNG, JPG, EXR, MP4, HAP, NotchLC, KTX2, etc.) from disk into a texture. Supports image sequences and looping.

key_params.name: file
key_params.note: File path to the image or video file

key_params.name: index
key_params.note: Frame index for image sequences or seek position in video — negative indices count from the end (2025.30000+)

key_params.name: reload
key_params.note: Pulse to force-reload the file from disk

key_params.name: preread
key_params.note: Pre-read frames ahead for smoother playback

key_params.name: predownload
key_params.note: 2025.30000+: download a remote/HTTP movie fully before playback starts

common_gotchas: HAP and NotchLC codecs offer GPU-decoded playback and are vastly faster than H.264 for real-time use.
Large image sequences can consume significant RAM — use pre-read carefully and monitor memory.
2025.30000+: .ktx (KTX2) format is now supported for compressed pre-baked textures.
Negative index values (2025.30000+) seek from the end — handy for cueing the last frame of a sequence without knowing the count.
